title: Phased cutover over single-weekend big-bang
context: All three applications must move to Northwind before 2026-12-31. OrderHub feeds Billing downstream. The sponsor (Mark) wants a single-weekend cutover of all three to take one business-disruption hit. The delivery team believes a phased sequence is materially safer because a single weekend would force three rollbacks to contend for the same window if anything goes wrong. A decision is needed at the 2026-06-10 steering so the runbook work can be finalized.
decision: We will cut over in phases: OrderHub first, a 2-week soak, then Billing and Reporting together. We will not attempt all three in one weekend.
consequences: Two maintenance weekends instead of one, so a second (smaller) business-disruption window is accepted. In exchange, each cutover gets a clean, uncontended rollback path, and problems found in the OrderHub soak inform the Billing/Reporting cutover. The schedule must still clear the 2026-12-31 backstop with the extra soak period included.
decision_drivers: OrderHub feeds Billing — a failed OrderHub cutover should not also jeopardize Billing in the same window
Rollback paths must not contend for one window
Must still clear the 2026-12-31 data-center backstop

options_considered.option: Phased: OrderHub first, soak, then Billing + Reporting

cons: Three rollbacks contend for one window
An OrderHub failure cascades into Billing immediately
